item.title: Active addiction vs 9 yrs in recovery!!
item.description:
Rock bottom April 3rd 2014
April 2022 Recovery is possible and every single person struggling out there deserves it and can achieve it ❤ Edit: Its hard for me to reply to everyone when I only have 140 characters. Heroin (fentanyl) was my drug of choice. Addiction runs heavy and deep on both sides of my family. My dad died of an overdose in 08. It hit me extremely hard. Thank God he died before I got strung out. Ill never stop thanking God for that. I got detained once in 13 and then full blown arrested in the first part of 14. The DA thankfully recommended treatment court at sentencing. I completed it in 2019. My county has an excellent program and our POs also are real hard asses. I needed that rigid structure at that time. It helped me maintain accountability. It also re taught me the life skills I was lacking. Not even a traffic violation after that lol What would I tell my former self: Theres not a damned thing I could tell myself during that time. Treatment court saved my life. No doubt abt it. At that point, death would have been a blessing to me. I kept my addiction hidden from public as long as I could. I was tired. Ready for change I couldnt bring abt on my own. I dont regret anything except the pain I caused my loved ones and my contribution to the overall shittiness of my community. I love and value myself 100% more now than I did before I even tried heroin. Heroin was never the problem. The problem was my lack of self;esteem and self worth. I wasted so much time making the mistakes it took to finally meet and love my real self. Thanks for all the love and support! No thanks to the ppl calling me ugly names. Ive seriously read some of the mean ones to my grandmother (who came to every single treatment court meeting with me) and were all laughing abt them Edit again: Thank you even more for all the positivity! To everyone in new sobriety, old sobriety, current addiction: You guys are in my thoughts tonight! Be gentle to yourselves and honestly take your time. With everything. If you dont arent there yet, youll grow with time and never forget how to appreciate even the littlest of things. To this day, I appreciate goosebumps that happen to me because the weather is actually cold not based on my withdrawal symptoms. I still focus on time hour by hour every now and then. Its a tool that actually works even though youve heard it preached a thousand times. You grow into it though. I did anyway. AA/NA and other step programs: I attended the meetings that were required of me. I learned something new each meeting. I researched the programs and gave them the respect they deserve. They werent for me but the lessons taught I retained and needed the non judgemental environment. I don't want to discourage anyone. Try and experience every time of step meeting you can attend at least once. Theyre def worth a try. 700 edits later: thats not a rifle in my backseat. Its a stupid old plastic nra lighter. I dont think it even works. Its good juju. Also, an ACTUAL AR-15 would take up my backseat. Please note the size in the photo.
item.dc:creator: Clydesayzso

item.title: I say this as someone with two degrees (almost three)
item.description: I’m a math teacher. Someone in a comment asked where factorials were used, and I mentioned that they’re used in probability and not much else (which is true). I also said that I’m in the middle of teaching my students about factorials, despite knowing most of them will never see it again (which is also true). You’d think I’d said that my students were all stupid and would never go anywhere in life, for how people read it. One person said I was dismissing both math and my students. Bizarre, given that I took 13 math classes after algebra 2 — which is what I take — for fun. I got two degrees so I could share my love of it with my students. Some of those students will be making more money than me in 5 years. And they’ll never see a factorial again. In fact, I’ll be so bold as to say that they’ll never take a math class again. AND THAT’S OKAY. The school where I work supports four postsecondary routes: college, trade school, workforce, and military. They all need to apply to the local community college, but it’s their choice to go. I’ve got kids going to school next year to be general contractors, mechanics, welders, roofers, and more. I’ve got a few who are already in apprentice programs. I’ve also got kids who are going to be going to work full time so they can help support their families. Some have kids of their own to support, some want to move out, and some have lost parents and siblings and need to pick up the slack. In none of these cases am I dismissing my students. They are, on the whole, wonderful, capable kids who will go on to contribute to society in one way or another. They are making smart financial decisions by avoiding student debt. They are getting jobs that can pay big bucks and developing useful skills and experience. Hell, I know a guy who dropped out at 14 and makes six figures driving a truck. Acknowledging what my students are doing with their futures is, in fact, the exact opposite of dismissing them. I’m currently working on getting them to be able to graduate early. I’ve already told one student from last year that I would commission a tattoo from them, and I have a student this year whose name I’m keeping in mind for when I buy a house and need to remodel. A girl in my first period is going to let me know where she ends up working as a mechanic. Another kid from last year is hustling to be a musician, working to buy studio time. They don’t need to go to college. They do t need more math class. They’ll be okay. Anecdotally, college also just isn’t for everyone. I went through severe trauma at 19 and had to take time off. I went back for a bit, and then ended up trying to off myself right before graduation because of the stress (it was partly a bad reaction to my medication, but the stress caused the breakdown). I’m taking this semester and next semester off from a masters program because trying to fight my ADHD and PTSD while also teaching and going to school was literally killing me. It wasn’t sustainable. I was getting three hours of sleep per night. Anyways, that’s my two cents. Agree, disagree, whatever. Edit: wHo ShItS oN tRaDe ScHoOl?? I hear it all the time from parents. I’m glad that y’all are having a different experience and don’t encounter it. Things may be swinging in the right direction, but I assure you, the idea that college is necessary is still out there in a big way.

item.title: Men Are Victims Too
item.description: A domestic survivor is finally taking her abuser to court. This poor woman had a cigarette put out on her face, a finger cut off, slaps and punches were part of her normal day, the man defecated in her bed while his friends laughed. There are audio and pictures to back all of this up. There were doctors that witnessed some of the abuse. The man put makeup on to imitate bruises for pictures and court appearances, but the next day there were no bruises to be seen￼. The man tried so so so hard to ruin this woman’s life. The media didn’t even ask her side. They blamed her drinking as the problem (she had been sober for YEARS but the violent relationship had led her back to her addiction, her only escape) the man leaked a picture of her asleep with ice cream melted on her to prove how “out of control” she was. To shame her even more. All of her past partners have stepped up to support her, all saying she was nothing but kind and loving. How can we treat domestic abuse survivors this way?? This woman isn’t a woman, she’s Johnny Depp, and the man is Amber Heard. For some reason his abuse wasn’t taken serious, it was mocked. If he were a woman would it have been so openly mocked? Abuse is Abuse. We need to protect victims regardless of gender. #JusticeForJohnnyDepp #AmberHeardIsAnAbuser
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: PSA: F— Elon Musk.
item.description: Someone with his status and wealth could accomplish so much, and yet he consistently chooses to be a petty man baby. And yeah, yeah - to everyone in the comments: I know it wasn’t to “end” world hunger for good. I’m not here to split hairs, I’m just here to point out one of the many monumental D-bags in the world. “To Safeguard Free Speech, We Must Hand Control of a Major Speech Platform to One Guy”: https://www.esquire.com/news-politics/a39813165/elon-musk-buy-twitter-censorship-democracy/ Oh yeah, this guy also wants to un-ban Trump in the name of freedom of speech. Give me a f—king break.

item.title: My Friend Likes to Send Me Science Stuff She Doesn’t Get
item.description: In no way am I qualified in the field of QFT, but I’ve watched and followed MIT and Stanford lectures on YouTube, and have been interested in theoretical physics for over a decade now. Hope I did my friend justice. @ Most viral edit: Ummmm … sooo… this is my first viral post… first off… thank you… second (noods?) idk obligatory?… third… invest your time in learning how to make this world better than the shit hole we are in the precipice of… love you.

Potato-carrot Rösti, Aioli Sour Cream From Berlinburger International In Berlin, Germany Previous Day 331: https://imgur.com/gallery/y3KRnb3 MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD @CrisprCAS Daily Tag
item.dc:creator: crypto

item.title: Satsuki Azalea ‘Nikkō’
item.description:
Approx 55 years old. Has been in my care for 10 years.
Nikkō is know for having multiple colors of flowers - white, pink and red.
The foliage pads grow their flower buds every July the year prior. Any work done on the tree must be done before July or done at the expense of blooms.
A show of white and pink - almost red flowers.
So. Many. Blooms.
Often blooms will show color break - when a bloom doesn’t have uniform color and here is the effect. A splash of red on pink.
item.dc:creator: ryan820

item.title: A Navajo man photographed in c. 1904 dressed as Nayenezgáni, a mythical hero from Navajo mythology who, along with his brother Tobadzischini, rid the world of the Anaye (monsters from Navajo mythology). Photograph taken by Edward S. Curtis in Navajo Natio

item.title: I got married today!!
item.description: I made my bouquet in the car on the way to the courthouse! We ate the strawberries immediately and the basil and rosemary I will be grinding down into some olive oil in the next few days! My *husband* (it makes me so giddy to write that!!!) returns to living out of the country this Wednesday, had popped the question back on Saturday, we filled out the paperwork online yesterday and ba bam - this morning we drove a few hours away and had a court ceremony! It’s all so very bittersweet…. I don’t really have much family but all of his has been reaching out to congratulate us and it’s so so wonderful!!!! I’m so happy and just needed an outlet to pour out my heart. Thank you for reading my post if you got this far :):):)
item.dc:creator: kittenspacecadet
